justification: UTK EECS faculty page for Lynne Parker documents her prior role as Division Director at NSF (Information and Intelligent Systems) 2015-2016, confirming the employer relationship.
Wikipedia article on Arati Prabhakar explicitly documents her serving as the 10th Director of NIST from 1993 to 1997, the first woman to hold the position.
Suresh Venkatasubramanian's personal University of Utah page explicitly identifies him as the John and Marva Warnock Assistant Professor at the School of Computing, University of Utah.
Eleos AI team page lists Robert Long as Executive Director, directly evidencing his employer relationship with Eleos AI.
Cornell Information Science faculty page for Claire Cardie identifies her as the John C. Ford Professor of Engineering in Computer Science and Information Science at Cornell University.
Official NYU news release announcing the Global AI Frontier Lab explicitly names Yann LeCun as co-director alongside Kyunghyun Cho.
TechCrunch article on AMI Labs explicitly names Alex LeBrun as CEO of Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs alongside Yann LeCun as executive chairman.
Brown CS news page explicitly names Ellie Pavlick as co-director leading ARIA, the NSF-funded AI Research Institute on Interaction for AI Assistants.
Alignment Forum post announcing the ACS Research Group explicitly names Jan Kulveit as founder and leader of the group, which is part of Charles University's Center for Theoretical Studies.
Hollywood Reporter article on Natasha Lyonne's AI company Asteria documents her as co-founder of Asteria Film Co.
TIME 100 AI 2025 profile of Ana Helena Ulbrich explicitly identifies her as co-founder of NoHarm and describes its role reviewing Brazilian hospital prescriptions.
RocketReach profile sourced from professional data identifies Stuart Russell as Founder and Vice-President of Bayesian Logic Inc., but this is a third-party aggregator rather than a primary source.
Official Mila press release on leadership transition explicitly names Yoshua Bengio as founder and notes his role as Scientific Director until 2025 when he became Scientific Advisor.
Wikipedia article on Element AI lists Yoshua Bengio among the co-founders of the Montreal-based AI company founded in October 2016.
IASEAI official conference page for Stuart Russell identifies him as President of the International Association for Safe and Ethical AI.
